volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: Oedipus at Colonus (Section 821a - Section 890)

sections.label: Section 821a
sections.translation: And I will have this one in no long time .

sections.label: Section 822
sections.original: ἰὼ ξένοι , τί δράσετʼ ; ἦ προδώσετε ,
sections.translation: Oh ! Strangers , what will you do ? Will you betray me ? Will you not drive the godless man from this land ?

sections.label: Section 824
sections.original: χώρει , ξένʼ , ἔξω θᾶσσον . οὔτε γὰρ τὰ νῦν
sections.translation: Depart , stranger ! Quick !

sections.label: Section 825
sections.original: δίκαια πράσσεις οὔθʼ ἃ πρόσθεν εἴργασαι .
sections.translation: Your present deed is not just , nor the deed which you have done .

sections.label: Section 826
sections.original: ὑμῖν ἂν εἴη τήνδε καιρὸς ἐξάγειν
sections.translation: It is time for you to drag this girl off against her will , if she will not go freely .

sections.label: Section 828
sections.original: οἴμοι τάλαινα , ποῖ φύγω ; ποίαν λάβω
sections.translation: Wretched that I am ! Where can I flee ? Where find help from gods or men ?

sections.label: Section 829a
sections.translation: What are you doing , stranger ?

sections.label: Section 830
sections.original: οὐχ ἅψομαι τοῦδʼ ἀνδρός , ἀλλὰ τῆς ἐμῆς .
sections.translation: I will not touch this man , but her who is mine .

sections.label: Section 831
sections.translation: Lords of the land !

sections.label: Section 831a
sections.translation: Stranger , you are acting unjustly .

sections.label: Section 832
sections.translation: Justly .

sections.label: Section 832a
sections.translation: How ?

sections.label: Section 832b
sections.translation: I take my own .

sections.label: Section 833
sections.translation: Oh , city !

sections.label: Section 834
sections.original: τί δρᾷς , ὦ ξένʼ ; οὐκ ἀφήσεις ; τάχʼ εἰς βάσανον εἶ χερῶν .
sections.translation: What are you doing , stranger ? Release her !

sections.label: Section 835
sections.translation: Your strength and ours will soon come to the test .

sections.label: Section 836
sections.original: πόλει μαχεῖ γάρ , εἴ τι πημανεῖς ἐμέ .
sections.translation: Stand back !

sections.label: Section 837
sections.translation: There will be war with Thebes for you , if you harm me .

sections.label: Section 838
sections.translation: Did I not say so ?

sections.label: Section 838a
sections.translation: Unhand the girl at once !

sections.label: Section 840
sections.translation: Let go , I tell you !

sections.label: Section 840a
sections.translation: And I tell you : be off !

sections.label: Section 841
sections.translation: Help , men of Colonus , bring help ! The city , our city , is attacked by force ! Come to our aid !

sections.label: Section 844
sections.translation: I am being dragged away in misery . Strangers , strangers !

sections.label: Section 845
sections.translation: My child , where are you ?

sections.label: Section 845a
sections.translation: I am led off by force .

sections.label: Section 846
sections.translation: Give me your hand , my child !

sections.label: Section 846a
sections.translation: I am helpless .

sections.label: Section 848
sections.original: οὔκουν ποτʼ ἐκ τούτοιν γε μὴ σκήπτροιν ἔτι
sections.translation: So those two staffs will never again support your path .

sections.label: Section 850
sections.original: πατρίδα τε τὴν σὴν καὶ φίλους , ὑφʼ ὧν ἐγὼ
sections.translation: But since you wish to overcome your country and your friends , whose will I , though tyrant as well , am here discharging , then I wish you victory . For in time , I am sure , you will come to recognize all this , that now too as in time past , it is you who have done yourself no good , by indulging your anger despite your friends .

sections.label: Section 855
sections.original: ὀργῇ χάριν δούς , ἥ σʼ ἀεὶ λυμαίνεται .
sections.translation: This has always been your ruin .

sections.label: Section 856
sections.translation: Stop there , stranger !

sections.label: Section 856a
sections.translation: Hands off , I say !

sections.label: Section 857
sections.original: οὔτοι σʼ ἀφήσω , τῶνδέ γʼ ἐστερημένος .
sections.translation: I will not let go , unless you give back the maidens .

sections.label: Section 858
sections.original: καὶ μεῖζον ἆρα ῥύσιον πόλει τάχα
sections.translation: Then you will soon give the city a more valuable prize , for I will lay hands on more than those two girls .

sections.label: Section 860
sections.translation: What ! What do you intend ?

sections.label: Section 860a
sections.translation: This man here will be my captive .

sections.label: Section 861
sections.translation: A valiant threat !

sections.label: Section 861a
sections.translation: Straightaway it will be done .

sections.label: Section 862
sections.original: ἢν μή σʼ ὁ κραίνων τῆσδε γῆς ἀπειργάθῃ .
sections.translation: Indeed , unless the ruler of this realm prevents you .

sections.label: Section 863
sections.original: ὦ φθέγμʼ ἀναιδές , ἦ σὺ γὰρ ψαύσεις ἐμοῦ ;
sections.translation: Voice of shamelessness ! Will you really lay hands on me ?

sections.label: Section 864
sections.translation: Shut up , I say !

sections.label: Section 864a
sections.translation: No ! May the powers of this place grant me to utter this further curse ! Most evil of men , when these eyes were dark , you wrenched from me the helpless one who was my eyesight and made off with her by force . Therefore to you and to your race may the Sun , the god who sees all things ,

sections.label: Section 870
sections.translation: grant in time an old age such as mine !

sections.label: Section 871
sections.translation: Do you see this , people of the land ?

sections.label: Section 872
sections.original: ὁρῶσι κἀμὲ καὶ σέ , καὶ φρονοῦσʼ ὅτι
sections.translation: They see both you and me . They know that I have suffered in deeds , and my defense is mere words .

sections.label: Section 874
sections.original: οὔτοι καθέξω θυμόν , ἀλλʼ ἄξω βίᾳ
sections.translation: I will not check my anger . Though I am alone

sections.label: Section 875
sections.original: κεἰ μοῦνός εἰμι τόνδε καὶ χρόνῳ βραδύς .
sections.translation: and slow with age , I will take this man by force .

sections.label: Section 876
sections.translation: Ah , my wretchedness !

sections.label: Section 877
sections.original: ὅσον λῆμʼ ἔχων ἀφίκου , ξένʼ , εἰ τάδε δοκεῖς τελεῖν .
sections.translation: What arrogance you have come with , stranger , if you think you will achieve this !

sections.label: Section 878
sections.translation: I will .

sections.label: Section 879
sections.translation: Then I think this city no longer exists .

sections.label: Section 880
sections.original: τοῖς τοι δικαίοις χὠ βραχὺς νικᾷ μέγαν .
sections.translation: For men who are just , you see , the weak vanquishes the strong .

sections.label: Section 881
sections.translation: Do you hear his words ?

sections.label: Section 881a
sections.translation: Yes , but he will not achieve them .

sections.label: Section 883
sections.original: Ζεύς γʼ ἂν εἰδείη , σὺ δʼ οὔ .
sections.translation: This is an outrage !

sections.label: Section 884
sections.translation: Hear people , hear rulers of the land ! Come quickly , come !

sections.label: Section 885
sections.original: ἰὼ πᾶς λεώς , ἰὼ γᾶς πρόμοι ,
sections.translation: These men are on their way to cross our borders !

sections.label: Section 887
sections.original: τίς ποθʼ ἡ βοή ; τί τοὔργον ; ἐκ τίνος φόβου ποτὲ
sections.translation: What is this shout ? What is the trouble ? What fear has moved you to stop my sacrifice at the altar to the sea-god , the lord of your Colonus ? Speak , so that I may know the situation ; for that is why I have sped

sections.label: Section 890
sections.original: οὗ χάριν δεῦρʼ ᾖξα θᾶσσον ἢ καθʼ ἡδονὴν ποδός .
sections.translation: here more swiftly than was pleasant .
